NKJV.1: Bless the LORD, O my soul! O LORD my God, You are very great: You are clothed with honor and majesty,
NKJV.2: Who cover Yourself with light as with a garment, Who stretch out the heavens like a curtain.
NKJV.3: He lays the beams of His upper chambers in the waters, Who makes the clouds His chariot, Who walks on the wings of the wind,
NKJV.4: Who makes His angels spirits, His ministers a flame of fire.
NKJV.5: You who laid the foundations of the earth, So that it should not be moved forever,
NKJV.6: You covered it with the deep as with a garment; The waters stood above the mountains.
NKJV.7: At Your rebuke they fled; At the voice of Your thunder they hastened away.
NKJV.8: They went up over the mountains; They went down into the valleys, To the place which You founded for them.
NKJV.9: You have set a boundary that they may not pass over,  That they may not return to cover the earth.
NKJV.10: He sends the springs into the valleys; They flow among the hills.
NKJV.11: They give drink to every beast of the field; The wild donkeys quench their thirst.
NKJV.12: By them the birds of the heavens have their home; They sing among the branches.
NKJV.13: He waters the hills from His upper chambers; The earth is satisfied with the fruit of Your works.
NKJV.14: He causes the grass to grow for the cattle, And vegetation for the service of man, That he may bring forth food from the earth,
NKJV.15: And wine that makes glad the heart of man, Oil to make his face shine, And bread which strengthens man’s heart.
NKJV.16: The trees of the LORD are full of sap, The cedars of Lebanon which He planted,
NKJV.17: Where the birds make their nests; The stork has her home in the fir trees.
NKJV.18: The high hills are for the wild goats; The cliffs are a refuge for the rock badgers.
NKJV.19: He appointed the moon for seasons; The sun knows its going down.
NKJV.20: You make darkness, and it is night, In which all the beasts of the forest creep about.
NKJV.21: The young lions roar after their prey, And seek their food from God.
NKJV.22: When the sun rises, they gather together And lie down in their dens.
NKJV.23: Man goes out to his work And to his labor until the evening.
NKJV.24: O LORD, how manifold are Your works! In wisdom You have made them all. The earth is full of Your possessions—
NKJV.25: This great and wide sea, In which are innumerable teeming things, Living things both small and great.
NKJV.26: There the ships sail about; There is that Leviathan Which You have made to play there.
NKJV.27: These all wait for You, That You may give them their food in due season.
NKJV.28: What You give them they gather in; You open Your hand, they are filled with good.
NKJV.29: You hide Your face, they are troubled;  You take away their breath, they die and return to their dust.
NKJV.30: You send forth Your Spirit, they are created; And You renew the face of the earth.
NKJV.31: May the glory of the LORD endure forever; May the LORD rejoice in His works.
NKJV.32: He looks on the earth, and it trembles;  He touches the hills, and they smoke.
NKJV.33: I will sing to the LORD as long as I live; I will sing praise to my God while I have my being.
NKJV.34: May my meditation be sweet to Him; I will be glad in the LORD.
NKJV.35: May sinners be consumed from the earth, And the wicked be no more. Bless the LORD, O my soul! Praise the LORD!

KJV.1: Bless<S>1288</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>, O my soul<S>5315</S>. O LORD<S>3068</S> my God<S>430</S>, thou art very<S>3966</S> great<S>1431</S>; thou art clothed<S>3847</S> with honour<S>1935</S> and majesty<S>1926</S>.

KJV.11: They give drink<S>8248</S> to every beast<S>2416</S> of the field<S>7704</S>: the wild asses<S>6501</S> quench<S>7665</S> their thirst<S>6772</S>. <sup>quench: Heb. break</sup>

KJV.15: And wine<S>3196</S> that maketh glad<S>8055</S> the heart<S>3824</S> of man<S>582</S>, and oil<S>8081</S> to make his face<S>6440</S> to shine<S>6670</S>, and bread<S>3899</S> which strengtheneth<S>5582</S> man's<S>582</S> heart<S>3824</S>. <sup>oil: Heb. to make his face shine with oil, or, more than oil</sup>

KJV.35: Let the sinners<S>2400</S> be consumed<S>8552</S> out of the earth<S>776</S>, and let the wicked<S>7563</S> be no more. Bless<S>1288</S> thou the LORD<S>3068</S>, O my soul<S>5315</S>. Praise<S>1984</S> ye the LORD<S>3050</S>.

ESV.1: Bless the LORD, O my soul! O LORD my God, you are very great! You are clothed with splendor and majesty,
ESV.2: covering yourself with light as with a garment, stretching out the heavens like a tent.
ESV.3: He lays the beams of his chambers on the waters; he makes the clouds his chariot; he rides on the wings of the wind;
ESV.4: he makes his messengers winds, his ministers a flaming fire.
ESV.5: He set the earth on its foundations, so that it should never be moved.
ESV.6: You covered it with the deep as with a garment; the waters stood above the mountains.
ESV.7: At your rebuke they fled; at the sound of your thunder they took to flight.
ESV.8: The mountains rose, the valleys sank down to the place that you appointed for them.
ESV.9: You set a boundary that they may not pass, so that they might not again cover the earth.
ESV.10: You make springs gush forth in the valleys; they flow between the hills;
ESV.11: they give drink to every beast of the field; the wild donkeys quench their thirst.
ESV.12: Beside them the birds of the heavens dwell; they sing among the branches.
ESV.13: From your lofty abode you water the mountains; the earth is satisfied with the fruit of your work.
ESV.14: You cause the grass to grow for the livestock and plants for man to cultivate, that he may bring forth food from the earth
ESV.15: and wine to gladden the heart of man, oil to make his face shine and bread to strengthen man's heart.
ESV.16: The trees of the LORD are watered abundantly, the cedars of Lebanon that he planted.
ESV.17: In them the birds build their nests; the stork has her home in the fir trees.
ESV.18: The high mountains are for the wild goats; the rocks are a refuge for the rock badgers.
ESV.19: He made the moon to mark the seasons;  the sun knows its time for setting.
ESV.20: You make darkness, and it is night, when all the beasts of the forest creep about.
ESV.21: The young lions roar for their prey, seeking their food from God.
ESV.22: When the sun rises, they steal away and lie down in their dens.
ESV.23: Man goes out to his work and to his labor until the evening.
ESV.24: O LORD, how manifold are your works! In wisdom have you made them all; the earth is full of your creatures.
ESV.25: Here is the sea, great and wide, which teems with creatures innumerable, living things both small and great.
ESV.26: There go the ships, and Leviathan, which you formed to play in it.
ESV.27: These all look to you, to give them their food in due season.
ESV.28: When you give it to them, they gather it up; when you open your hand, they are filled with good things.
ESV.29: When you hide your face, they are dismayed; when you take away their breath, they die and return to their dust.
ESV.30: When you send forth your Spirit,  they are created, and you renew the face of the ground.
ESV.31: May the glory of the LORD endure forever; may the LORD rejoice in his works,
ESV.32: who looks on the earth and it trembles, who touches the mountains and they smoke!
ESV.33: I will sing to the LORD as long as I live; I will sing praise to my God while I have being.
ESV.34: May my meditation be pleasing to him, for I rejoice in the LORD.
ESV.35: Let sinners be consumed from the earth, and let the wicked be no more! Bless the LORD, O my soul! Praise the LORD!

NASB.1: Bless the LORD, O my soul! O LORD my God, You are very great; You are clothed with splendor and majesty,
NASB.2: Covering Yourself with light as with a cloak, Stretching out heaven like a [tent] curtain.
NASB.3: He lays the beams of His upper chambers in the waters; He makes the clouds His chariot; He walks upon the wings of the wind;
NASB.4: He makes the winds His messengers, Flaming fire His ministers.
NASB.5: He established the earth upon its foundations, So that it will not totter forever and ever.
NASB.6: You covered it with the deep as with a garment; The waters were standing above the mountains.
NASB.7: At Your rebuke they fled, At the sound of Your thunder they hurried away.
NASB.8: The mountains rose; the valleys sank down To the place which You established for them.
NASB.9: You set a boundary that they may not pass over, So that they will not return to cover the earth.
NASB.10: He sends forth springs in the valleys; They flow between the mountains;
NASB.11: They give drink to every beast of the field; The wild donkeys quench their thirst.
NASB.12: Beside them the birds of the heavens dwell; They lift up [their] voices among the branches.
NASB.13: He waters the mountains from His upper chambers; The earth is satisfied with the fruit of His works.
NASB.14: He causes the grass to grow for the cattle, And vegetation for the labor of man, So that he may bring forth food from the earth,
NASB.15: And wine which makes man's heart glad, So that he may make [his] face glisten with oil, And food which sustains man's heart.
NASB.16: The trees of the LORD drink their fill, The cedars of Lebanon which He planted,
NASB.17: Where the birds build their nests, [And] the stork, whose home is the fir trees.
NASB.18: The high mountains are for the wild goats; The cliffs are a refuge for the shephanim.
NASB.19: He made the moon for the seasons; The sun knows the place of its setting.
NASB.20: You appoint darkness and it becomes night, In which all the beasts of the forest prowl about.
NASB.21: The young lions roar after their prey And seek their food from God.
NASB.22: [When] the sun rises they withdraw And lie down in their dens.
NASB.23: Man goes forth to his work And to his labor until evening.
NASB.24: O LORD, how many are Your works! In wisdom You have made them all; The earth is full of Your possessions.
NASB.25: There is the sea, great and broad, In which are swarms without number, Animals both small and great.
NASB.26: There the ships move along, [And] Leviathan, which You have formed to sport in it.
NASB.27: They all wait for You To give them their food in due season.
NASB.28: You give to them, they gather [it] up; You open Your hand, they are satisfied with good.
NASB.29: You hide Your face, they are dismayed; You take away their spirit, they expire And return to their dust.
NASB.30: You send forth Your Spirit, they are created; And You renew the face of the ground.
NASB.31: Let the glory of the LORD endure forever; Let the LORD be glad in His works;
NASB.32: He looks at the earth, and it trembles; He touches the mountains, and they smoke.
NASB.33: I will sing to the LORD as long as I live; I will sing praise to my God while I have my being.
NASB.34: Let my meditation be pleasing to Him; As for me, I shall be glad in the LORD.
NASB.35: Let sinners be consumed from the earth And let the wicked be no more. Bless the LORD, O my soul. Praise the LORD!

NLT.1:
Let all that I am praise the LORD.
O LORD my God, how great you are!
You are robed with honor and majesty.
NLT.2:
You are dressed in a robe of light.
You stretch out the starry curtain of the heavens;
NLT.3:
you lay out the rafters of your home in the rain clouds.
You make the clouds your chariot;
you ride upon the wings of the wind.
NLT.4:
The winds are your messengers;
flames of fire are your servants.
NLT.5:
You placed the world on its foundation
so it would never be moved.
NLT.6:
You clothed the earth with floods of water,
water that covered even the mountains.
NLT.7:
At your command, the water fled;
at the sound of your thunder, it hurried away.
NLT.8:
Mountains rose and valleys sank
to the levels you decreed.
NLT.9:
Then you set a firm boundary for the seas,
so they would never again cover the earth.
NLT.10:
You make springs pour water into the ravines,
so streams gush down from the mountains.
NLT.11:
They provide water for all the animals,
and the wild donkeys quench their thirst.
NLT.12:
The birds nest beside the streams
and sing among the branches of the trees.
NLT.13:
You send rain on the mountains from your heavenly home,
and you fill the earth with the fruit of your labor.
NLT.14:
You cause grass to grow for the livestock
and plants for people to use.
You allow them to produce food from the earth —
NLT.15:
wine to make them glad,
olive oil to soothe their skin,
and bread to give them strength.
NLT.16:
The trees of the LORD are well cared for —
the cedars of Lebanon that he planted.
NLT.17:
There the birds make their nests,
and the storks make their homes in the cypresses.
NLT.18:
High in the mountains live the wild goats,
and the rocks form a refuge for the hyraxes.
NLT.19:
You made the moon to mark the seasons,
and the sun knows when to set.
NLT.20:
You send the darkness, and it becomes night,
when all the forest animals prowl about.
NLT.21:
Then the young lions roar for their prey,
stalking the food provided by God.
NLT.22:
At dawn they slink back
into their dens to rest.
NLT.23:
Then people go off to their work,
where they labor until evening.
NLT.24:
O LORD, what a variety of things you have made!
In wisdom you have made them all.
The earth is full of your creatures.
NLT.25:
Here is the ocean, vast and wide,
teeming with life of every kind,
both large and small.
NLT.26:
See the ships sailing along,
and Leviathan, which you made to play in the sea.
NLT.27:
They all depend on you
to give them food as they need it.
NLT.28:
When you supply it, they gather it.
You open your hand to feed them,
and they are richly satisfied.
NLT.29:
But if you turn away from them, they panic.
When you take away their breath,
they die and turn again to dust.
NLT.30:
When you give them your breath, life is created,
and you renew the face of the earth.
NLT.31:
May the glory of the LORD continue forever!
The LORD takes pleasure in all he has made!
NLT.32:
The earth trembles at his glance;
the mountains smoke at his touch.
NLT.33:
I will sing to the LORD as long as I live.
I will praise my God to my last breath!
NLT.34:
May all my thoughts be pleasing to him,
for I rejoice in the LORD.
NLT.35:
Let all sinners vanish from the face of the earth;
let the wicked disappear forever.
Let all that I am praise the LORD.
Praise the LORD!

WEB.1: Bless Yahweh, my soul. Yahweh, my God, you are very great. You are clothed with honor and majesty.
WEB.2: He covers himself with light as with a garment. He stretches out the heavens like a curtain.
WEB.3: He lays the beams of his rooms in the waters. He makes the clouds his chariot. He walks on the wings of the wind.
WEB.4: He makes his messengers  winds; his servants flames of fire.
WEB.5: He laid the foundations of the earth, that it should not be moved forever.
WEB.6: You covered it with the deep as with a cloak. The waters stood above the mountains.
WEB.7: At your rebuke they fled. At the voice of your thunder they hurried away.
WEB.8: The mountains rose, the valleys sank down, to the place which you had assigned to them.
WEB.9: You have set a boundary that they may not pass over; that they don’t turn again to cover the earth.
WEB.10: He sends springs into the valleys. They run among the mountains.
WEB.11: They give drink to every animal of the field. The wild donkeys quench their thirst.
WEB.12: The birds of the sky nest by them. They sing among the branches.
WEB.13: He waters the mountains from his rooms. The earth is filled with the fruit of your works.
WEB.14: He causes the grass to grow for the livestock, and plants for man to cultivate, that he may produce food out of the earth:
WEB.15: wine that makes glad the heart of man, oil to make his face to shine, and bread that strengthens man’s heart.
WEB.16: Yahweh’s trees are well watered, the cedars of Lebanon, which he has planted;
WEB.17: where the birds make their nests. The stork makes its home in the cypress trees.
WEB.18: The high mountains are for the wild goats. The rocks are a refuge for the rock badgers.
WEB.19: He appointed the moon for seasons. The sun knows when to set.
WEB.20: You make darkness, and it is night, in which all the animals of the forest prowl.
WEB.21: The young lions roar after their prey, and seek their food from God.
WEB.22: The sun rises, and they steal away, and lay down in their dens.
WEB.23: Man goes out to his work, to his labor until the evening.
WEB.24: Yahweh, how many are your works! In wisdom have you made them all. The earth is full of your riches.
WEB.25: There is the sea, great and wide, in which are innumerable living things, both small and large animals.
WEB.26: There the ships go, and leviathan, whom you formed to play there.
WEB.27: These all wait for you, that you may give them their food in due season.
WEB.28: You give to them; they gather. You open your hand; they are satisfied with good.
WEB.29: You hide your face: they are troubled; you take away their breath: they die, and return to the dust.
WEB.30: You send out your Spirit and they are created. You renew the face of the ground.
WEB.31: Let Yahweh’s glory endure forever. Let Yahweh rejoice in his works.
WEB.32: He looks at the earth, and it trembles. He touches the mountains, and they smoke.
WEB.33: I will sing to Yahweh as long as I live. I will sing praise to my God while I have any being.
WEB.34: Let my meditation be sweet to him. I will rejoice in Yahweh.
WEB.35: Let sinners be consumed out of the earth. Let the wicked be no more. Bless Yahweh, my soul. Praise Yah!

mountains rose and valleys sank [2]  —
to the place you established for them. ⓑ
CSB17.9:
You set a boundary they cannot cross;
they will never cover the earth again. ⓒ
CSB17.10:
He causes the springs to gush into the valleys;
they flow between the mountains. ⓓ
CSB17.11:
They supply water for every wild beast;
the wild donkeys quench their thirst. ⓔ
CSB17.12:
The birds of the sky live beside the springs;
they make their voices heard among the foliage. ⓕ
CSB17.13:
He waters the mountains from his palace;
the earth is satisfied by the fruit of your labor. ⓖ
CSB17.14:
He causes grass to grow for the livestock
and provides crops for man to cultivate,
producing food from the earth, ⓗ
CSB17.15:
wine that makes human hearts glad —
making his face shine with oil —
and bread that sustains human hearts. ⓘ
CSB17.16:
The trees of the LORD flourish, [3]
the cedars of Lebanon that he planted. ⓙ
CSB17.17:
There the birds make their nests;
storks make their homes in the pine trees. ⓚ
CSB17.18:
The high mountains are for the wild goats;
the cliffs are a refuge for hyraxes. ⓛ
CSB17.19:
He made the moon to mark the [4] festivals; [5]
the sun knows when to set. ⓜ
CSB17.20:
You bring darkness, and it becomes night,
when all the forest animals stir. ⓝ
CSB17.21:
The young lions roar for their prey
and seek their food from God. ⓐ
CSB17.22:
The sun rises; they go back
and lie down in their dens. ⓑ
CSB17.23:
Man goes out to his work
and to his labor until evening. ⓒ
CSB17.24:
How countless are your works, LORD!
In wisdom you have made them all;
the earth is full of your creatures. [6] ⓓ
CSB17.25:
Here is the sea, vast and wide,
teeming with creatures beyond number —
living things both large and small. ⓔ
CSB17.26:
There the ships move about,
and Leviathan, which you formed to play there. ⓕ
CSB17.27:
All of them wait for you
to give them their food at the right time. ⓖ
CSB17.28:
When you give it to them,
they gather it;
when you open your hand,
they are satisfied with good things. ⓗ
CSB17.29:
When you hide your face,
they are terrified;
when you take away their breath,
they die and return to the dust. ⓘ
CSB17.30:
When you send your breath, [7]
they are created,
and you renew the surface of the ground. ⓙ
CSB17.31:
May the glory of the LORD endure forever;
may the LORD rejoice in his works. ⓚ
CSB17.32:
He looks at the earth, and it trembles;
he touches the mountains,
and they pour out smoke. ⓛ
CSB17.33:
I will sing to the LORD all my life;
I will sing praise to my God while I live. ⓜ
CSB17.34:
May my meditation be pleasing to him;
I will rejoice in the LORD. ⓝ
CSB17.35:
May sinners vanish from the earth
and wicked people be no more. ⓐ
My soul, bless the LORD!
Hallelujah!

AMP.1:
Bless and affectionately praise the LORD, O my soul!
O LORD my God, You are very great;
You are clothed with splendor and majesty,
AMP.2:
[You are the One] who covers Yourself with light as with a garment,
Who stretches out the heavens like a tent curtain,
AMP.3:
Who lays the beams of His upper chambers in the waters [above the firmament],
Who makes the clouds His chariot,
Who walks on the wings of the wind,
AMP.4:
Who makes winds His messengers,
Flames of fire His ministers.
AMP.5:
He established the earth on its foundations,
So that it will not be moved forever and ever.
AMP.6:
You covered it with the deep as with a garment;
The waters were standing above the mountains.
AMP.7:
At Your rebuke they fled;
At the sound of Your thunder they hurried away.
AMP.8:
The mountains rose, the valleys sank down
To the place which You established for them.
AMP.9:
You set a boundary [for the waters] that they may not cross over,
So that they will not return to cover the earth.
AMP.10:
You send springs into the valleys;
Their waters flow among the mountains.
AMP.11:
They give drink to every beast of the field;
The wild donkeys quench their thirst there.
AMP.12:
Beside them the birds of the heavens have their nests;
They lift up their voices and sing among the branches.
AMP.13:
He waters the mountains from His upper chambers;
The earth is satisfied with the fruit of His works.
AMP.14:
He causes grass to grow for the cattle,
And all that the earth produces for cultivation by man,
So that he may bring food from the earth—
AMP.15:
And wine which makes the heart of man glad,
So that he may make his face glisten with oil,
And bread to sustain and strengthen man's heart.
AMP.16:
The trees of the LORD drink their fill,
The cedars of Lebanon which He has planted,
AMP.17:
Where the birds make their nests;
As for the stork, the fir trees are her house.
AMP.18:
The high mountains are for the wild goats;
The rocks are a refuge for the shephanim.
AMP.19:
He made the moon for the seasons;
The sun knows the [exact] place of its setting.
AMP.20:
You [O LORD] make darkness and it becomes night,
In which prowls about every wild beast of the forest.
AMP.21:
The young lions roar after their prey
And seek their food from God.
AMP.22:
When the sun arises, they withdraw
And lie down in their dens.
AMP.23:
Man goes out to his work
And remains at his labor until evening.
AMP.24:
O LORD, how many and varied are Your works!
In wisdom You have made them all;
The earth is full of Your riches and Your creatures.
AMP.25:
There is the sea, great and broad,
In which are swarms without number,
Creatures both small and great.
AMP.26:
There the ships [of the sea] sail,
And Leviathan [the sea monster], which You have formed to play there.
AMP.27:
They all wait for You
To give them their food in its appointed season.
AMP.28:
You give it to them, they gather it up;
You open Your hand, they are filled and satisfied with good [things].
AMP.29:
You hide Your face, they are dismayed;
You take away their breath, they die
And return to their dust.
AMP.30:
You send out Your Spirit, they are created;
You renew the face of the ground.
AMP.31:
May the glory of the LORD endure forever;
May the LORD rejoice and be glad in His works—
AMP.32:
He looks at the earth, and it trembles;
He touches the mountains, and they smoke.
AMP.33:
I will sing to the LORD as long as I live;
I will sing praise to my God while I have my being.
AMP.34:
May my meditation be sweet and pleasing to Him;
As for me, I will rejoice and be glad in the LORD.
AMP.35:
Let sinners be consumed from the earth,
And let the wicked be no more.
Bless and affectionately praise the LORD, O my soul.
Praise the LORD! (Hallelujah!)

MSG.1:
O my soul, bless God!
God, my God, how great you are!
beautifully, gloriously robed,
MSG.2:
Dressed up in sunshine,
and all heaven stretched out for your tent.
MSG.3:
You built your palace on the ocean deeps,
made a chariot out of clouds and took off on wind-wings.
MSG.4:
You commandeered winds as messengers,
appointed fire and flame as ambassadors.
MSG.5:
You set earth on a firm foundation
so that nothing can shake it, ever.
MSG.6:
You blanketed earth with ocean,
covered the mountains with deep waters;
MSG.7:
Then you roared and the water ran away—
your thunder crash put it to flight.
MSG.8:
Mountains pushed up, valleys spread out
in the places you assigned them.
MSG.9:
You set boundaries between earth and sea;
never again will earth be flooded.
MSG.10:
You started the springs and rivers,
sent them flowing among the hills.
MSG.11:
All the wild animals now drink their fill,
wild donkeys quench their thirst.
MSG.12:
Along the riverbanks the birds build nests,
ravens make their voices heard.
MSG.13:
You water the mountains from your heavenly cisterns;
earth is supplied with plenty of water.
MSG.14:
You make grass grow for the livestock,
hay for the animals that plow the ground.
Oh yes, God brings grain from the land,
MSG.15:
wine to make people happy,
Their faces glowing with health,
a people well-fed and hearty.
MSG.16:
God's trees are well-watered—
the Lebanon cedars he planted.
MSG.17:
Birds build their nests in those trees;
look—the stork at home in the treetop.
MSG.18:
Mountain goats climb about the cliffs;
badgers burrow among the rocks.
MSG.19:
The moon keeps track of the seasons,
the sun is in charge of each day.
MSG.20:
When it's dark and night takes over,
all the forest creatures come out.
MSG.21:
The young lions roar for their prey,
clamoring to God for their supper.
MSG.22:
When the sun comes up, they vanish,
lazily stretched out in their dens.
MSG.23:
Meanwhile, men and women go out to work,
busy at their jobs until evening.
MSG.24:
What a wildly wonderful world, God!
You made it all, with Wisdom at your side,
made earth overflow with your wonderful creations.
MSG.25:
Oh, look—the deep, wide sea,
brimming with fish past counting,
sardines and sharks and salmon.
MSG.26:
Ships plow those waters,
and Leviathan, your pet dragon, romps in them.
MSG.27:
All the creatures look expectantly to you
to give them their meals on time.
MSG.28:
You come, and they gather around;
you open your hand and they eat from it.
MSG.29:
If you turned your back,
they'd die in a minute—
Take back your Spirit and they die,
revert to original mud;
MSG.30:
Send out your Spirit and they spring to life—
the whole countryside in bloom and blossom.
MSG.31:
The glory of God—let it last forever!
Let God enjoy his creation!
MSG.32:
He takes one look at earth and triggers an earthquake,
points a finger at the mountains, and volcanoes erupt.
MSG.33:
Oh, let me sing to God all my life long,
sing hymns to my God as long as I live!
MSG.34:
Oh, let my song please him;
I'm so pleased to be singing to God.
MSG.35:
But clear the ground of sinners—
no more godless men and women!
O my soul, bless God!

NIV.1:
Psalm 104
Praise the Lord, O my soul.
O Lord my God, you are very great;
you are clothed with splendor and majesty.
NIV.2:
He wraps himself in light as with a garment;
he stretches out the heavens like a tent
NIV.3:
and lays the beams of his upper chambers on their waters.
He makes the clouds his chariot
and rides on the wings of the wind.
NIV.4:
He makes winds his messengers,
flames of fire his servants.
NIV.5:
He set the earth on its foundations;
it can never be moved.
NIV.6:
You covered it with the deep as with a garment;
the waters stood above the mountains.
NIV.7:
But at your rebuke the waters fled,
at the sound of your thunder they took to flight;
NIV.8:
they flowed over the mountains,
they went down into the valleys,
to the place you assigned for them.
NIV.9:
You set a boundary they cannot cross;
never again will they cover the earth.
NIV.10:
He makes springs pour water into the ravines;
it flows between the mountains.
NIV.11:
They give water to all the beasts of the field;
the wild donkeys quench their thirst.
NIV.12:
The birds of the air nest by the waters;
they sing among the branches.
NIV.13:
He waters the mountains from his upper chambers;
the earth is satisfied by the fruit of his work.
NIV.14:
He makes grass grow for the cattle,
and plants for man to cultivate—
bringing forth food from the earth:
NIV.15:
wine that gladdens the heart of man,
oil to make his face shine,
and bread that sustains his heart.
NIV.16:
The trees of the Lord are well watered,
the cedars of Lebanon that he planted.
NIV.17:
There the birds make their nests;
the stork has its home in the pine trees.
NIV.18:
The high mountains belong to the wild goats;
the crags are a refuge for the coneys.
NIV.19:
The moon marks off the seasons,
and the sun knows when to go down.
NIV.20:
You bring darkness, it becomes night,
and all the beasts of the forest prowl.
NIV.21:
The lions roar for their prey
and seek their food from God.
NIV.22:
The sun rises, and they steal away;
they return and lie down in their dens.
NIV.23:
Then man goes out to his work,
to his labor until evening.
NIV.24:
How many are your works, O Lord!
In wisdom you made them all;
the earth is full of your creatures.
NIV.25:
There is the sea, vast and spacious,
teeming with creatures beyond number—
living things both large and small.
NIV.26:
There the ships go to and fro,
and the leviathan, which you formed to frolic there.
NIV.27:
These all look to you
to give them their food at the proper time.
NIV.28:
When you give it to them,
they gather it up;
when you open your hand,
they are satisfied with good things.
NIV.29:
When you hide your face,
they are terrified;
when you take away their breath,
they die and return to the dust.
NIV.30:
When you send your Spirit,
they are created,
and you renew the face of the earth.
NIV.31:
May the glory of the Lord endure forever;
may the Lord rejoice in his works—
NIV.32:
he who looks at the earth, and it trembles,
who touches the mountains, and they smoke.
NIV.33:
I will sing to the Lord all my life;
I will sing praise to my God as long as I live.
NIV.34:
May my meditation be pleasing to him,
as I rejoice in the Lord.
NIV.35:
But may sinners vanish from the earth
and the wicked be no more.
Praise the Lord, O my soul.
Praise the Lord.

NRSVCE.1:
Bless the LORD, O my soul.
O LORD my God, you are very great.
You are clothed with honor and majesty,
NRSVCE.2:
wrapped in light as with a garment.
You stretch out the heavens like a tent,
NRSVCE.3:
you set the beams of your chambers on the waters,
you make the clouds your chariot,
you ride on the wings of the wind,
NRSVCE.4:
you make the winds your messengers,
fire and flame your ministers.
NRSVCE.5:
You set the earth on its foundations,
so that it shall never be shaken.
NRSVCE.6:
You cover it with the deep as with a garment;
the waters stood above the mountains.
NRSVCE.7:
At your rebuke they flee;
at the sound of your thunder they take to flight.
NRSVCE.8:
They rose up to the mountains, ran down to the valleys
to the place that you appointed for them.
NRSVCE.9:
You set a boundary that they may not pass,
so that they might not again cover the earth.
NRSVCE.10:
You make springs gush forth in the valleys;
they flow between the hills,
NRSVCE.11:
giving drink to every wild animal;
the wild asses quench their thirst.
NRSVCE.12:
By the streams the birds of the air have their habitation;
they sing among the branches.
NRSVCE.13:
From your lofty abode you water the mountains;
the earth is satisfied with the fruit of your work.
NRSVCE.14:
You cause the grass to grow for the cattle,
and plants for people to use,
to bring forth food from the earth,
NRSVCE.15:
and wine to gladden the human heart,
oil to make the face shine,
and bread to strengthen the human heart.
NRSVCE.16:
The trees of the LORD are watered abundantly,
the cedars of Lebanon that he planted.
NRSVCE.17:
In them the birds build their nests;
the stork has its home in the fir trees.
NRSVCE.18:
The high mountains are for the wild goats;
the rocks are a refuge for the coneys.
NRSVCE.19:
You have made the moon to mark the seasons;
the sun knows its time for setting.
NRSVCE.20:
You make darkness, and it is night,
when all the animals of the forest come creeping out.
NRSVCE.21:
The young lions roar for their prey,
seeking their food from God.
NRSVCE.22:
When the sun rises, they withdraw
and lie down in their dens.
NRSVCE.23:
People go out to their work
and to their labor until the evening.
NRSVCE.24:
O LORD, how manifold are your works!
In wisdom you have made them all;
the earth is full of your creatures.
NRSVCE.25:
Yonder is the sea, great and wide,
creeping things innumerable are there,
living things both small and great.
NRSVCE.26:
There go the ships,
and Leviathan that you formed to sport in it.
NRSVCE.27:
These all look to you
to give them their food in due season;
NRSVCE.28:
when you give to them, they gather it up;
when you open your hand, they are filled with good things.
NRSVCE.29:
When you hide your face, they are dismayed;
when you take away their breath, they die
and return to their dust.
NRSVCE.30:
When you send forth your spirit, they are created;
and you renew the face of the ground.
NRSVCE.31:
May the glory of the LORD endure forever;
may the LORD rejoice in his works —
NRSVCE.32:
who looks on the earth and it trembles,
who touches the mountains and they smoke.
NRSVCE.33:
I will sing to the LORD as long as I live;
I will sing praise to my God while I have being.
NRSVCE.34:
May my meditation be pleasing to him,
for I rejoice in the LORD.
NRSVCE.35:
Let sinners be consumed from the earth,
and let the wicked be no more.
Bless the LORD, O my soul.
Praise the LORD!

NET.1: Praise the LORD, O my soul! O LORD my God, you are magnificent. You are robed in splendor and majesty.
NET.2: He covers himself with light as if it were a garment. He stretches out the skies like a tent curtain,
NET.3: and lays the beams of the upper rooms of his palace on the rain clouds. He makes the clouds his chariot, and travels along on the wings of the wind.
NET.4: He makes the winds his messengers, and the flaming fire his attendant.
NET.5: He established the earth on its foundations; it will never be upended.
NET.6: The watery deep covered it like a garment; the waters reached above the mountains.
NET.7: Your shout made the waters retreat; at the sound of your thunderous voice they hurried off —
NET.8: as the mountains rose up, and the valleys went down — to the place you appointed for them.
NET.9: You set up a boundary for them that they could not cross, so that they would not cover the earth again.
NET.10: He turns springs into streams; they flow between the mountains.
NET.11: They provide water for all the animals in the field; the wild donkeys quench their thirst.
NET.12: The birds of the sky live beside them; they chirp among the bushes.
NET.13: He waters the mountains from the upper rooms of his palace; the earth is full of the fruit you cause to grow.
NET.14: He provides grass for the cattle, and crops for people to cultivate, so they can produce food from the ground,
NET.15: as well as wine that makes people feel so good, and so they can have oil to make their faces shine, as well as food that sustains people’s lives.
NET.16: The trees of the LORD receive all the rain they need, the cedars of Lebanon which he planted,
NET.17: where the birds make nests, near the evergreens in which the herons live.
NET.18: The wild goats live in the high mountains; the rock badgers find safety in the cliffs.
NET.19: He made the moon to mark the months, and the sun sets according to a regular schedule.
NET.20: You make it dark and night comes, during which all the beasts of the forest prowl around.
NET.21: The lions roar for prey, seeking their food from God.
NET.22: When the sun rises, they withdraw and sleep in their dens.
NET.23: Men then go out to do their work, and labor away until evening.
NET.24: How many living things you have made, O LORD! You have exhibited great skill in making all of them; the earth is full of the living things you have made.
NET.25: Over here is the deep, wide sea, which teems with innumerable swimming creatures, living things both small and large.
NET.26: The ships travel there, and over here swims the whale you made to play in it.
NET.27: All of your creatures wait for you to provide them with food on a regular basis.
NET.28: You give food to them and they receive it; you open your hand and they are filled with food.
NET.29: When you ignore them, they panic. When you take away their life’s breath, they die and return to dust.
NET.30: When you send your life-giving breath, they are created, and you replenish the surface of the ground.
NET.31: May the splendor of the LORD endure! May the LORD find pleasure in the living things he has made!
NET.32: He looks down on the earth and it shakes; he touches the mountains and they start to smolder.
NET.33: I will sing to the LORD as long as I live; I will sing praise to my God as long as I exist!
NET.34: May my thoughts be pleasing to him! I will rejoice in the LORD.
NET.35: May sinners disappear from the earth, and the wicked vanish! Praise the LORD, O my soul! Praise the LORD!
